name: Inguinal hernia anatomy and repair

figure_scopes.overview: Inguinal anatomy with inferior epigastric vessels as the orienting landmark
figure_scopes.storyboard: Overview -> direct vs indirect paths -> repair/mesh plane -> final covered anatomy
figure_scopes.closeup: Direct vs indirect relationship to the inferior epigastric vessels

laterality: Inguinal region; deep ring lies lateral to the inferior epigastric vessels, Hesselbach triangle lies medial to them
must_have: Inferior epigastric vessels, deep inguinal ring, spermatic cord or round ligament, direct space, and indirect hernia path.
Hesselbach triangle boundaries when direct hernia anatomy is shown.
Mesh plane or myopectineal orifice only if the request asks for repair anatomy.
spatial_rules: Indirect hernia is lateral to the inferior epigastric vessels and follows the deep ring / inguinal canal path.
Direct hernia is medial to the inferior epigastric vessels within Hesselbach triangle.
Mesh coverage should span direct, indirect, and femoral potential spaces when repair anatomy is shown.

forbidden_errors.error: Direct and indirect hernia positions swapped relative to the inferior epigastric vessels.
Mesh run through cord structures, vessels, or nerves.
Inferior epigastric vessels omitted in a comparison figure.

safety_concept: Use the inferior epigastric vessels as the landmark separating direct (medial) from indirect (lateral) hernias.
phases: Inguinal anatomy overview with inferior epigastric vessels.
Repair plane or mesh coverage concept, if requested.
verify: Direct/indirect relationship to the inferior epigastric vessels is correct.
Deep ring and cord/round ligament relationship is coherent.
Mesh, if present, covers spaces without crossing through vital structures.
